description: Address semantic review feedback: (1) Use IClassFixture<T> to share template synthesis across tests in EndToEndValidationTest.cs, (2) Fix Python test import pattern to use module-level import, (3) Update README troubleshooting to mention xunit.runner.json parallel disablement.
steps: Create a fixture class (EndToEndValidationFixture) that synthesizes the Template once and caches the state machine JSON string, then make EndToEndValidationTest implement IClassFixture<EndToEndValidationFixture> and remove per-test CreateTemplate() calls
Refactor test_index.py to import the index module at top level and call index.handler(event, context) instead of using from index import handler inside each test method
Update README.md Troubleshooting section to note that xunit.runner.json already disables parallel execution to prevent JSII resource conflicts
acceptance_criteria: EndToEndValidationTest uses IClassFixture<T> pattern and synthesizes template only once for all 56 tests
Python test_index.py imports index at top level and calls index.handler() consistently
README troubleshooting mentions xunit.runner.json parallel execution configuration
verification: unset NODE_OPTIONS && dotnet test src/CdkBase.sln --verbosity normal
findings: All changes implemented successfully. IClassFixture pattern reduced template synthesis from 56x to 1x - EndToEndValidation tests now run in <1ms each instead of ~150ms+. Python tests pass with top-level import pattern. README troubleshooting section updated.
